title: Compass Explorer
instructions_for_student: Watch the compass needle settle, predict which way it points, then drag the box to align N with the needle. Try at all three locations!

toolSchema.description: An interactive 5-step compass learning tool. Students watch a realistic SVG compass needle swing and settle (per-location RAF physics animation), predict north, drag to align the dial, then explore multiple map locations — proving the needle always points north regardless of location.
toolSchema.textbook: NCERT Curiosity — Textbook of Science, Grade 6

1.title: Watch the Needle Settle
1.description: Student observes the compass needle swing from a displaced position and settle pointing north. Each location has a unique swing personality (amplitude, frequency, decay).

2.title: Make Your Prediction
2.description: Student selects which direction the red needle tip is pointing. Four options: North / South / East / West. Correct answer is always North.

3.title: Align the Compass
3.description: Student drag-rotates the compass box until N on the dial lines up with the red needle. Live alignment meter shows degrees off. Success triggers green glow + direction reveal grid.

4.title: Explore Every Location
4.description: Unified map+compass screen. Student taps each pin on the illustrated SVG map — compass resets with a new unique swing — and aligns N at each location. Compass is draggable inline without navigating away.

5.title: Summary
5.description: Key concept cards: Earth is a giant magnet, North-seeking pole, compass works everywhere, reading N/S/E/W from the dial. Restart button.

title.description: Main H1 heading displayed at top of the tool. Rendered as gradient text using design system colors.

subtitle.description: Small blue pill chip displayed above the title. Use for chapter or activity reference.

locations.description: Array of 2–5 Location objects. Each gets a unique needle-swing personality and fixed dial start offset so every location feels dramatically different. Built-in IDs (school/forest/river) match illustrated map terrain art.

id.description: Unique key. Use school | forest | river for built-in terrain art. Any string works for custom locations.

name.description: Display name shown on map pin label, active location badge, and step descriptions.

emoji.description: Single emoji for the map pin icon. Use thematically appropriate emojis.

x.description: X position as % of map width. Note: built-in IDs use hardcoded safe pin coordinates regardless of this value.

y.description: Y position as % of map height.

color.description: Hex color for active pin, badge border, location row highlight. Each location should have a visually distinct color.

needlePersonalities.description: Each location index receives a fixed swing personality. Personalities are assigned by array index, not by location ID.

personalities.feel: Wide, lazy pendulum — N starts far left
Fast tight oscillation — N starts near bottom-right (almost flipped)
Dramatic slow pendulum — biggest swing, slowest decay

responsive.layoutMobile: Single column, full-width stacked, full-width buttons, 44px tap targets
responsive.layoutTablet: Single column, centred compass, medium padding
responsive.layoutDesktop: Side-by-side row: compass on left, info/guidance panel on right

explore.description: Single linear 5-step flow. No branching, no mode tabs.

agentReadme._note: Agent-facing documentation. Not rendered by the tool.
agentReadme.overview: CompassExplorerTool is a 5-step interactive compass learning experience for NCERT Grade 6 Chapter 4. Students observe a physically realistic RAF-animated compass needle, predict north, drag-align the dial, then explore 2–5 map locations — proving the needle always points north regardless of location. The tool is fully responsive (fluid compass size, CSS media-query layout) and follows the Singularity design system (Poppins, #4A4DC9, gradient #533086→#FC9145).

quickReferenceTable.studentRequest: Teach me how a compass works

showStepIndicator.description: Show/hide the 5 progress dots at the top of the card

showModeSelector.description: Always false — this tool has one linear flow, no mode tabs

showNavigation.description: Always false — steps advance via in-step CTA buttons

showPlayPause.description: Always false — student-paced interaction, no autoplay

animationSpeed.description: Multiplier for animation durations. 0.5 = twice as fast.

title.description: Main heading. Rendered in gradient text (#533086 → #4A4DC9 → #FC9145).

subtitle.description: Pill chip above title. Use for chapter/activity reference.

locations.description: 2–5 Location objects. Order determines needle-swing personality (index 0 = wide slow, 1 = fast tight, 2 = dramatic slow).

id.note: Built-in: school|forest|river → matched terrain art. Custom: any string → fallback coords.

x.note: 0–100, % of map width. Ignored for built-in IDs.

y.note: 0–100, % of map height. Ignored for built-in IDs.

color.note: Hex color. Each location should be visually distinct.

example1_default._description: Default 3 locations — textbook school, forest, river
example1_default.title: Compass Explorer
example1_default.instructions_for_student: Watch the needle settle, predict North, then drag the compass box to align — at all three locations!

example2_activity_subtitle._description: Activity 4.3 framing — same locations, textbook activity subtitle
example2_activity_subtitle.title: Activity 4.3 — Finding Directions
example2_activity_subtitle.instructions_for_student: A freely suspended magnet always points north–south. Use the compass to find directions at each location!

example3_two_locations.title: Compass Basics
example3_two_locations.instructions_for_student: Can you find North in the classroom AND the garden? Test your compass!

example4_four_locations._description: Extended exploration — 4 varied locations around a town
example4_four_locations.title: Compass Around Town
example4_four_locations.instructions_for_student: Explore 4 different locations. Does the compass always agree on which way is north?

example5_five_locations.title: North Is Everywhere
example5_five_locations.instructions_for_student: Challenge: align the compass at all 5 locations. The needle never lies!

example6_kerala_village._description: Village/Kerala context — connects to the Reshma textbook story
example6_kerala_village.title: Finding North in Kerala
example6_kerala_village.instructions_for_student: Reshma wants to navigate from the backwaters to the coconut grove. Help her align the compass at each location!

example7_sailor_theme._description: Sailor/ocean theme — connects to textbook's ship-spices story
example7_sailor_theme.title: Sailor's Compass
example7_sailor_theme.instructions_for_student: In the olden days, sailors used a magnetic compass to navigate oceans. Align the compass at each waypoint on the voyage!

example8_assessment._description: Assessment/proof framing — student must demonstrate mastery
example8_assessment.title: Prove It: Compass Works Everywhere
example8_assessment.instructions_for_student: Your task: align the compass at all 3 locations and observe — does the needle always point the same way? Why?

example9_compact_embed._description: Compact embed for sidebar or iframe — reduced size, 2 locations
example9_compact_embed.title: Quick Compass Check
example9_compact_embed.instructions_for_student: Align the compass at both locations.

example10_custom_title_only._description: Only change title — use defaults for everything else
example10_custom_title_only.title: Where Is North?
example10_custom_title_only.instructions_for_student: The compass needle always tells you where north is — test it everywhere!

branches.note: Full 3-location default — covers all textbook content for Activity 4.3

branches.keyChange: Title framed as a task, subtitle says Assessment

branches.condition: Just change the title / chapter reference
branches.keyChange: Only modify title and subtitle strings

tipsForAgents: Always include at least 2 locations — the map step requires minimum 2 pins to be meaningful.
Use built-in IDs (school / forest / river) to get matching illustrated terrain art on the map (river water, forest trees, school building). Custom IDs use fallback pin coordinates.
Needle swing personality is determined by array INDEX (0, 1, 2…), not by ID — reordering locations changes which swing each location gets.
The compass size is fluid (clamp(160px, 35vw, 280px)) — no need to set width/height unless embedding in a constrained container.
For textbook alignment, set subtitle to the specific activity reference: 'Activity 4.3 — Finding Directions'.
The correct answer to step 2 (prediction) is ALWAYS North — this is fixed by the physics, not configurable.
All 5 steps always run in order. filterSteps is not supported — the flow is linear by design.
Each location's color should be visually distinct. Avoid similar hues in the same locations array.
For assessment contexts, frame the title/subtitle as a challenge/task and include an observation question in instructions_for_student.
Step 4 is fully self-contained — students can stay on the map screen and align multiple locations without navigating away.

errorHandling.lessThan2Locations: Renders with 1 location but step 4 map is less engaging (only 1 pin). Use at least 2.
errorHandling.moreThan5Locations: Locations beyond index 4 use fallback personality and map coords — may overlap on the map.
errorHandling.emptyAdditionalProps: All defaults apply — title 'Compass Explorer', subtitle 'Chapter 4 — Exploring Magnets', 3 default locations.
errorHandling.unknownLocationId: Works fine — custom IDs use interpolated map coordinates (x/y % values) instead of hardcoded terrain positions.
errorHandling.duplicateIds: Only the first occurrence renders — ensure all location IDs are unique.

learningObjectives: Understand that a freely suspended bar magnet always aligns north–south
Identify the red end of the needle as the North-seeking pole
Understand that Earth behaves like a giant magnet
Learn to use a magnetic compass to find cardinal directions
Observe that the compass works identically at every location on Earth
Practice aligning a compass dial with the needle to read N, S, E, W
